questions.question: The following sentence has been split into four segments. Identify the segment that contains a grammatical error.
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (B) Replace "is" with "are". 📌 Concept: Nouns with Two Parts (Plural in Form and Function) – Certain nouns in English describe items that are made of two parts working together. These nouns are always treated as plural and require a plural verb. Even though "scissors" refers to a single tool, grammatically it is not a singular entity. These nouns are always used as plural: Spectacles, Scissors, Pliers, Tweezers, Tongs, Shears, Glasses, Pants, Jeans, Trousers, Shorts, Leggings, Binoculars etc. Corrected Sentence: The Spectacles are on the table.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank. The CEO decided to ________ the annual conference due to the sudden travel restrictions affecting all international speakers.
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (B). 📌 Explanation - Put off means to delay or postpone an event or activity until a later time, which fits the context of rescheduling a conference due to restrictions. Meanings and Examples of Options: Bring up: उल्लेख करना / पालन-पोषण करना -To mention a subject or to care for a child until they are an adult. Example: Please don't bring up the budget issue during the meeting. Put off: स्थगित करना / टालना - To cancel something that was planned, especially an event or an agreement to a later date. Example: The wedding was put off until next summer. Carry on: जारी रखना - To continue doing something. Example: Despite the noise, she tried to carry on with her work. Break down: खराब होना / टूट जाना - To stop functioning (for machinery) or to lose emotional control. Example: The car broke down in the middle of the highway.

questions.question: Select the option that will improve the underlined part of the given sentence. In case no improvement is needed, select 'No improvement required'. The young entrepreneur was obsessed for the idea of creating a sustainable fashion brand that used only recycled materials.
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (A). 📌 Explanation - The underlined part "obsessed for" is incorrect because the word obsessed is an adjective that takes the fixed preposition 'with'. In English grammar, when you want to say that someone's mind is completely filled with thoughts of a particular person or thing, you must use obsessed with. Obsessed with - किसी चीज़ के लिए जुनूनी होना - To be preoccupied with something to a troubling or excessive degree. Example: Many teenagers today are completely obsessed with social media trends.

questions.question: Select the Grammatically correct sentence -
options: The number of qualified applicants have increased today.
Many a brilliant scholar have won this prize.
Each of the three local banks is solvent.
More candidate than one are waiting for you.
questions.explanation: 👉 The Correct Ans is Option (C)- Each of the three local banks is solvent. 📌 Explanation - This sentence is grammatically correct because it follows the rule of Subject-Verb Agreement with "Each of". The phrase "Each of" is always singular in nature, regardless of the plural noun (banks) that follows it. Therefore, the main verb of the sentence must be singular (is). Explanation for other wrong options: Option A: Error in Subject-Verb Agreement with "The number of". The phrase "The number of" denotes a specific total or statistic and is always considered singular in nature. So, replace "have" with "has." Option B: Error in Subject-Verb Agreement with "Many a". The structure "Many a" focuses on individuals one by one. It must always be followed by a singular noun and a singular verb. So, replace "have" with "has." Option D: Error in Noun Number with "More... than one". When using the structure "More [Noun] than one", if the noun immediately follows the word "More", it must be in the plural form. So, replace the singular "candidate" with the plural "candidates."

questions.question: Select the option that will improve the underlined part of the given sentence. In case no improvement is needed, select 'No improvement required'. The high-altitude training program focuses on increasing lung capacity, building core strength, and to improve overall endurance.
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (A). 📌 Explanation – The underlined part "to improve overall endurance" is incorrect because it violates the rule of Parallel Structure. When you list items in a sentence, they must all follow the same grammatical pattern. Grammatical concept: Parallelism In this sentence, the first two items in the list use the Gerund form (Verb + ing): 1. increasing lung capacity 2. building core strength 3. Therefore, the third item must also be a Gerund (improving) to match the pattern, rather than an infinitive ("to improve"). Example: He likes swimming, hiking, and jogging (not "to jog").

questions.question: Choose the most appropriate option to make the sentence sensibly correct. The detective's __________ investigation eventually led to the discovery of the hidden documents that had been missing for decades.
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (B). 📌 Explanation - Meticulous is the most appropriate fit because it describes someone who shows great attention to detail and is very careful and precise—essentially a "thorough" or "detailed" investigation. In the context of finding hidden documents missing for decades, it highlights the careful and exhaustive nature of the work. Meanings and explanation of other options: Cursory: सतही / जल्दबाजी में किया गया - Hasty and therefore not thorough or detailed. Example: He gave the report a cursory glance before signing it. Negligent: लापरवाह / असावधान - Failing to take proper care in doing something. Example: The council was found negligent in its duty to maintain the public park. Laconic: संक्षिप्त / मितभाषी - Using very few words (used for a person, speech, or style of writing). Example: His laconic reply suggested a lack of interest in the conversation.

questions.question: Identify the segment in the sentence that contains grammatical error.
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (3). 📌 Replace "the hikers already left" with "the hikers HAD already left" Error concept: This sentence uses the phrase "By the time," which almost always requires a sequence of tenses using the Past Perfect. Grammatical concept: When one action is completed before a specific point in the past (in this case, the arrival of the rescue team), the earlier action must be in the Past Perfect ($had + V3$). Action 1 (Earlier): Hikers leaving. (Should be: had already left) Action 2 (Later): Rescue team reaching the site. (Correct: reached) Correct Sentence: By the time the rescue team reached the site, the hikers had already left.

questions.question: Select the sentence containing the homonym of the highlighted word: The director was finally content with the final cut of the film.
options: She sat by the fire, feeling peaceful and content.
The baby fell asleep, looking quite content after his meal.
The table of content helped the reader find the specific chapter.
They were content to stay at home instead of going to the party.
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (C). 📌 Explanation: In the given sentence, Content is an adjective meaning— in a state of peaceful happiness or satisfaction. A, B, D In these sentences, content is used as an adjective to describe a person’s state of being satisfied or happy, which is the same meaning as the original sentence. C. "The table of content helped the reader..." Here, content is used as a noun meaning the things that are held or included in something (like a book). This is a different meaning/usage. So, the correct homonym is option (C).

questions.question: Arrange the sentences in the correct order to form a meaningful paragraph. (A) These include the development of critical thinking, problem-solving, and effective communication. (B) Consequently, prioritizing these competencies in higher education is vital for preparing students for the modern workforce. (C) Soft skills are a cluster of personal qualities that enable someone to interact effectively with others. (D) In the absence of these essential attributes, even highly technical professionals often find it difficult to lead teams or manage conflicts.
questions.explanation: 👉 The Correct Option is (A) - CADB 📌 Explanation - Step-by-Step Logic The Opening (Sentence C): This sentence introduces and defines the main topic ("Soft skills"). It is an independent statement that provides the foundation for the rest of the passage. The Elaboration (Sentence A): This starts with "These include...", which serves as a pointer to the "personal qualities" mentioned in Sentence C. It provides specific examples (critical thinking, communication). [Link: C-A] The Consequence (Sentence D): This sentence explains what happens "In the absence of these essential attributes" (referring back to the skills in C and A). It highlights the struggle of professionals. [Link: A-D] The Conclusion (Sentence B): It begins with the transition word "Consequently," offering a solution (prioritizing them in education) to the problems mentioned in the previous sentences. [Link: D-B] FINAL PARAGRAPH: Soft skills are a cluster of personal qualities that enable someone to interact effectively with others. These include the development of critical thinking, problem-solving, and effective communication. In the absence of these essential attributes, even highly technical professionals often find it difficult to lead teams or manage conflicts. Consequently, prioritizing these competencies in higher education is vital for preparing students for the modern workforce.

questions.question: Choose the most appropriate option to change the voice of the given sentence. It was suggested by Drashti that the files should be delayed by the clerk.
options: Drashti suggested that the clerk should delay the files.
Drashti suggested the clerk to delay the files.
Drashti suggested that the files should be delayed by the clerk.
Drashti suggested that the clerk delayed the files.
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (A). 📌 Explanation - This question tests your ability to identify the correct subject-verb relationship and maintain modal consistency when converting a complex sentence from Passive to Active voice. Subject Identification: In the passive sentence, "Drashti" is the agent introduced by the preposition by. In the active voice, Drashti must move to the front to become the primary subject performing the action. Clause Structure: The verb "suggested" is an intransitive verb that usually requires a "that" clause. When converting the second part of the sentence (the files should be delayed by the clerk), the "clerk" must be moved to the subject position of that specific clause. Modal Consistency: The modal verb "should" expresses a recommendation or suggestion. It must be retained in the active voice to keep the original meaning intact. Option (D) is incorrect because it replaces the modal with a simple past verb ("delayed"), changing a suggestion into a statement of fact. The Infinitive Trap: Option (B) is grammatically incorrect. In formal English, the verb "suggest" cannot be followed by an object and an infinitive (e.g., suggested the clerk to...). It requires either a gerund or a "that" clause. Option (C) is incorrect because it fails to convert the second half of the sentence, leaving it in the passive voice.

questions.question: Change the narration of the given sentence. Rutu said to me, "I am writing a letter to my friend."
options: Rutu told me that she is writing a letter to her friend.
Rutu said to me that she was writing a letter to my friend.
Rutu told me that she was writing a letter to her friend.
Rutu told me that I was writing a letter to her friend.
questions.explanation: 👉The correct answer is Option C: Rutu told me that she was writing a letter to her friend. 📌 Explanation- This question tests your ability to apply standard backshift rules and pronoun consistency when converting an assertive sentence into Indirect Speech. Reporting Verb Conversion: The phrase "said to" must be changed to "told" because there is a direct object ("me") receiving the speech. Option (B) is incorrect because it retains the direct structure. Tense Consistency: In reported speech, if the reporting verb is in the past ("said"), the Present Continuous ("am writing") must backshift to the Past Continuous ("was writing"). Option (A) is incorrect because it fails to change the tense. Pronoun Accuracy: The first-person subject "I" refers to the speaker (Rutu), so it must change to "she". The possessive adjective "my" refers to Rutu's friend, so it must change to "her". Option (D) is incorrect because it incorrectly changes the subject of the sentence to "I," which alters the meaning of who is performing the action.

questions.question: The fisherfolk of Thaikkadappuram beach in Nileshwaram, located in Kerala's Kasaragod district, play with Olive Ridley turtle hatchlings before releasing them into the ___ (1) ___. They treat the hatchlings with affection and are fully aware of the ecological importance of ___ (2) ___ turtles. Active participants in the region's turtle conservation efforts, these fisherfolk have helped save numerous eggs. Neithal, an organization that has been working ___ (3) ___ the conservation of Olive Ridleys for the past 25 years at Thaikkadappuram, has succeeded in creating awareness among the coastal population on the need to protect these turtle eggs. Sudheer Kumar PV, a biology teacher and the co-founder and secretary of Neithal, says people are ___ (4) ____ to inform volunteers if they spot turtles or eggs. Most of them stand guard until the volunteers reach the spot. Over the years, Neithal has successfully protected, hatched and released over 35,000 Olive Ridley hatchlings. Through camps for children and the public, they ___ (5) ____ about an awareness on marine turtles in the region and the need for protecting them. Choose the most appropriate option to fill in the blank 1 -
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (B): sea. 📌Explanation: The paragraph begins by mentioning "Thaikkadappuram beach" and concludes by discussing "marine turtles." Since Olive Ridley turtles are a marine species and beaches are coastal features, the "sea" is the only logically consistent body of water for their release. Why other options are wrong: * "Lake," "river," and "pond" are freshwater bodies. The context clues "beach" and "marine" specifically point toward a saltwater environment (the sea).

questions.question: The fisherfolk of Thaikkadappuram beach in Nileshwaram, located in Kerala's Kasaragod district, play with Olive Ridley turtle hatchlings before releasing them into the ___ (1) ___. They treat the hatchlings with affection and are fully aware of the ecological importance of ___ (2) ___ turtles. Active participants in the region's turtle conservation efforts, these fisherfolk have helped save numerous eggs. Neithal, an organization that has been working ___ (3) ___ the conservation of Olive Ridleys for the past 25 years at Thaikkadappuram, has succeeded in creating awareness among the coastal population on the need to protect these turtle eggs. Sudheer Kumar PV, a biology teacher and the co-founder and secretary of Neithal, says people are ___ (4) ____ to inform volunteers if they spot turtles or eggs. Most of them stand guard until the volunteers reach the spot. Over the years, Neithal has successfully protected, hatched and released over 35,000 Olive Ridley hatchlings. Through camps for children and the public, they ___ (5) ____ about an awareness on marine turtles in the region and the need for protecting them. Choose the most appropriate option to fill in the blank 2 -
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (C): these. 📌 Explanation: The noun "turtles" is plural, which immediately eliminates "this" and "that." We use "these" to refer to a plural noun that has just been mentioned in the immediate context (the "hatchlings" from the previous sentence). Why other options are wrong: "This" and "That" are singular demonstrative adjectives and cannot be used with the plural noun "turtles." "Those" is used for plural nouns that are distant in space or time; however, since the passage is discussing a specific group of turtles currently being described, "these" is the grammatically appropriate choice for immediate reference.

questions.question: The fisherfolk of Thaikkadappuram beach in Nileshwaram, located in Kerala's Kasaragod district, play with Olive Ridley turtle hatchlings before releasing them into the ___ (1) ___. They treat the hatchlings with affection and are fully aware of the ecological importance of ___ (2) ___ turtles. Active participants in the region's turtle conservation efforts, these fisherfolk have helped save numerous eggs. Neithal, an organization that has been working ___ (3) ___ the conservation of Olive Ridleys for the past 25 years at Thaikkadappuram, has succeeded in creating awareness among the coastal population on the need to protect these turtle eggs. Sudheer Kumar PV, a biology teacher and the co-founder and secretary of Neithal, says people are ___ (4) ____ to inform volunteers if they spot turtles or eggs. Most of them stand guard until the volunteers reach the spot. Over the years, Neithal has successfully protected, hatched and released over 35,000 Olive Ridley hatchlings. Through camps for children and the public, they ___ (5) ____ about an awareness on marine turtles in the region and the need for protecting them. Choose the most appropriate option to fill in the blank 3 -
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (D): towards 📌 Explanation: In this context, "working" is used to describe an effort aimed at a specific goal. The preposition "towards" is the standard idiomatic choice when describing progress or labor directed at achieving a result like "conservation." Why other options are wrong: "Against" would imply the organisation is trying to stop conservation, which contradicts the entire passage. "At" usually refers to a specific location or a specific skill level (e.g., "working at the beach"), but it doesn't fit the goal-oriented nature of the sentence. "Forward" is an adverb; while you can "move forward," you don't "work forward the conservation" without a preposition like "on" or "with."

questions.question: The fisherfolk of Thaikkadappuram beach in Nileshwaram, located in Kerala's Kasaragod district, play with Olive Ridley turtle hatchlings before releasing them into the ___ (1) ___. They treat the hatchlings with affection and are fully aware of the ecological importance of ___ (2) ___ turtles. Active participants in the region's turtle conservation efforts, these fisherfolk have helped save numerous eggs. Neithal, an organization that has been working ___ (3) ___ the conservation of Olive Ridleys for the past 25 years at Thaikkadappuram, has succeeded in creating awareness among the coastal population on the need to protect these turtle eggs. Sudheer Kumar PV, a biology teacher and the co-founder and secretary of Neithal, says people are ___ (4) ____ to inform volunteers if they spot turtles or eggs. Most of them stand guard until the volunteers reach the spot. Over the years, Neithal has successfully protected, hatched and released over 35,000 Olive Ridley hatchlings. Through camps for children and the public, they ___ (5) ____ about an awareness on marine turtles in the region and the need for protecting them. Choose the most appropriate option to fill in the blank 4 -
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (C): prompt. 📌 Explanation: The word "prompt" means to act quickly and without delay. The context of the paragraph shows that the community is "active" and they even "stand guard" until volunteers arrive. This level of dedication indicates a rapid, enthusiastic response rather than a slow or unwilling one. Why other options are wrong: "Hesitant" and "Reluctant" both imply a lack of will or a feeling of doubt, which contradicts the "awareness" and "affection" the fisherfolk have for the turtles. "Delayed" suggests a late response, whereas the passage emphasizes the effectiveness of their conservation efforts.

questions.question: The fisherfolk of Thaikkadappuram beach in Nileshwaram, located in Kerala's Kasaragod district, play with Olive Ridley turtle hatchlings before releasing them into the ___ (1) ___. They treat the hatchlings with affection and are fully aware of the ecological importance of ___ (2) ___ turtles. Active participants in the region's turtle conservation efforts, these fisherfolk have helped save numerous eggs. Neithal, an organization that has been working ___ (3) ___ the conservation of Olive Ridleys for the past 25 years at Thaikkadappuram, has succeeded in creating awareness among the coastal population on the need to protect these turtle eggs. Sudheer Kumar PV, a biology teacher and the co-founder and secretary of Neithal, says people are ___ (4) ____ to inform volunteers if they spot turtles or eggs. Most of them stand guard until the volunteers reach the spot. Over the years, Neithal has successfully protected, hatched and released over 35,000 Olive Ridley hatchlings. Through camps for children and the public, they ___ (5) ____ about an awareness on marine turtles in the region and the need for protecting them. Choose the most appropriate option to fill in the blank 5 -
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (A): brought. 📌 Explanation: The phrase "brought about" is a phrasal verb that means to cause something to happen or to make something happen. In this context, it refers to the organization causing a change in the public's level of awareness. Why other options are wrong: "Carried about" is not a standard idiomatic expression for causing change; "carried out" would mean to execute a task, but it doesn't fit with the preposition "about." "Thought about" means to consider something, but the sentence describes an active result of the camps, not just a mental process. "Took about" is not a standard phrasal verb used in this context.

questions.question: Identify the word that is correctly spelt.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is – Gregarious. Let’s see the meaning of all words: 1. Correct Spelling: Cumbersome - बोझिल - difficult to handle or manage Example: The cumbersome package was hard to carry. 2. Correct Spelling: Deferential - आदरपूर्ण - showing respect or courtesy Example: She was deferential towards her elders. 3. Correct Spelling: Gregarious - मिलनसार - sociable or friendly Example: He's a gregarious person and loves parties. 4. Correct Spelling: - Peculiar - अजीब - unusual or unexpected Example: The peculiar smell made her curious.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate synonym of the underlined word in the following sentence - The sense of happiness he felt after clearing the competitive examination was fleeting, as it was soon replaced by anxiety about interviews, document verification, and the uncertainty of final selection.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is – Transient. Let’s see the meaning of all words: Fleeting - क्षणिक - lasting for a very short time Example: The sense of happiness he felt after clearing the competitive examination was soon replaced by anxiety. Enduring - स्थायी - lasting for a long time Example: Her love for music is enduring and inspires her kids. Transient - अस्थायी - lasting for a short time, temporary Example: The transient nature of life is a common theme in poetry. Tyro - नवसिखुआ - a beginner or novice Example: As a tyro, she was eager to learn the ropes. Paucity - कमी - a lack or scarcity of something Example: The project suffered due to a paucity of funds.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate antonym of the given word. MALLEABLE
questions.explanation: The correct answer is – Inflexible. Let’s see the meaning of all words: Malleable - अनुकूल - capable of being shaped or influenced Example: The sculptor expertly molded the malleable wax into intricate designs, crafting a masterpiece that would be showcased at the international art exhibition. Inflexible - अनम्य - not capable of being shaped or influenced Example: The bureaucratic system's inflexible protocols impeded the implementation of innovative solutions, hindering the organization's ability to adapt to the rapidly changing market dynamics. Sagacious - बुद्धिमान - having keen discernment Example: The sagacious entrepreneur's astute decision to diversify her investments proved prescient, as the market fluctuations ultimately vindicated her prudent strategy. Intractable - अड़ियल - difficult to control or influence Example: The intractable conflict between the rival factions seemed insurmountable, with deep-seated animosities and entrenched positions threatening to derail the fragile peace process. Pliant - लम्य - flexible or adaptable Example: The pliant nature of the newly developed material allowed it to absorb immense pressure without cracking, making it an ideal component for the construction of earthquake-resistant buildings.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning for the given idiom. Butterflies in the stomach
options: From all parts of the world
Experiencing anxiety or apprehension about an event
questions.explanation: The correct answer is - Experiencing anxiety or apprehension about an event Let’s see the meaning of idiom: Butterflies in the stomach - पेट में मचलना - feeling nervous or anxious Example: She had butterflies in her stomach before the big interview.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate SYNONYM of the given word. RECOLLECT
questions.explanation: The correct answer is – Retrieve. Let’s see the meaning of all words: Recollect - याद करना - to remember or bring to mind Example: She tried to recollect the details of the accident. Tactful - विनम्र - having a keen sense of what to say or do Example: He was tactful in his approach to the sensitive topic. Retrieve - पुनः प्राप्त करना - to get or bring something back Example: She retrieved her phone from her bag. Stingy - कंजूस - unwilling to give or share Example: He's stingy with his money, never spends a dime. Vagrant - भटकने वाला - a person without a fixed home or job Example: The vagrant slept on the park bench.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate antonym of the underlined word in the following sentence- The students were granted extra time to submit their assignments as a matter of courtesy, despite the strict deadline announced earlier.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is – Rudeness. Let’s see the meaning of all words: Courtesy - विनम्रता - polite behavior or respect Example: The staff showed courtesy to the customers. Moist - गीला - slightly wet Example: The soil was moist after the rain. Rudeness - अशिष्टता - lack of politeness or respect Example: His rudeness towards the guest was unacceptable. Felony - गंभीर अपराध - a serious crime Example: Theft is considered a felony in many countries. Weary - थका हुआ - feeling tired Example: She felt weary after a long day's work.

questions.question: Select the word which means the same as the group of words. Fit to be eaten
questions.explanation: The correct answer is – Palatable. Let’s see the meaning of all words: Pesky - परेशान करने वाला - annoying or troublesome Example: The pesky mosquitoes ruined the picnic. Ornate - अलंकृत - decorated or elaborate Example: The ornate decorations added to the room's elegance. Nippy - तीखा - biting or sharp in taste or manner Example: The nippy weather made us bundle up. Palatable - स्वादिष्ट - pleasant to eat or tasty, fit to be eaten Example: The palatable soup was a hit at the party.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom. Despite contributing significantly to the project, he was left out in the cold when promotions were announced and credit was distributed among the team.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is – to be ignored or neglected. Let’s see the meaning of idiom: Left out in the cold- अनदेखा या उपेक्षित होना - to be ignored, neglected, or excluded Example: Despite his efforts, he was left out in the cold during the promotion round.

questions.question: Select the word which means the same as the group of words given. One who has suddenly gained new wealth, power or prestige
questions.explanation: The correct answer is – Upstart. Let’s see the meaning of all words: Incarnate - मूर्त रूप - having a particular quality or characteristic Example: She's kindness incarnate. Rustic - ग्रामीण - relating to the countryside or rural areas Example: The rustic charm of the village attracted tourists. Upstart - नवागंतुक - a person who has recently gained success or power Example: The upstart company took the market by storm. Verger - चर्च का अधिकारी - a person who carries a rod or staff before a church official Example: The verger led the procession.

questions.question: Which of the following is the top-ranked country in the World Press Freedom Index 2025?
questions.explanation: Norway is the top-ranked country in the World Press Freedom Index 2025. India ranked 151th. Additional information: The World Press Freedom Index is an annual ranking published by Reporters Without Borders (RSF), evaluating the level of press freedom in 180 countries and territories. It assesses factors like pluralism, media independence, the legal framework, journalist safety, and economic conditions for media.

questions.question: Special procedure in respect of Money Bills is described in which article of Indian constitution?
questions.explanation: Article 109 of the Indian Constitution deals with the special procedure in respect of Money Bills. It outlines the limited role of the Rajya Sabha (Council of States) in the passage of Money Bills, ensuring the primacy of the Lok Sabha (House of the People) in financial matters. The definition of a Money Bill is provided in Article 110 of the Indian Constitution.

questions.question: Which of the following is a pseudocoelomate animal?
questions.explanation: Pseudocoelomate = Nematoda= Ascaris(Roundworm) true coelom in Annelida, Arthropoda etc. Additional information: Acoelomate animals- Porifera, Coelenterate, Ctenophora, Platihelminhes. Pseudocoelomate- Nematoda. Eucoelomate- Annelida, Aschelminthes, Mollusca, Echinodermata, Chordata etc.

questions.question: Which of the following statements is correct regarding the allotropes of carbon?
options: Diamond is a good conductor of electricity due to the presence of delocalized electrons in its structure.
Graphite acts as a lubricant because of weak van der Waals forces between its hexagonal layers.
Fullerene (C₆₀) is the hardest known allotrope of carbon and is used in cutting tools.
Graphene is a three-dimensional allotrope of carbon with sp³ hybridization.
questions.explanation: Option (A) is wrong: Diamond is an insulator (no free electrons; all in covalent bonds), while graphite is a conductor (delocalized π-electrons). Common trap: confusing conductivity. Option (C) is wrong: Fullerene is not the hardest (diamond is hardest due to tetrahedral sp³ network); fullerene is spherical and used in nanotechnology/drugs. Option (D) is wrong: Graphene is a single layer (2D) of graphite with sp² hybridization, not 3D. Only (B) is accurate: Graphite's slippery nature comes from weak interlayer forces allowing layers to slide

questions.question: Which of the following statements about Aramaic language is/are correct? 1.Aramaic is a language related to Hebrew and Arabic. 2.Aramaic language has been used in Ashokan inscriptions. Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.explanation: 1. Aramaic is a language related to Hebrew and Arabic. 2. Aramaic language has been used in Ashokan inscriptions. Both statements are true. Hence option C is correct. Additional information: Total language used: 3 Total scripts used: 4 Most inscription were in: Pakrit language in Brahmi script Script used in Afghanisthan: Greek and Aramaic Script used Northwestern parts: Kharosthi

questions.question: Which of the following pairs of musician and their primary gharana is incorrect?
questions.explanation: Pandit Ravi Shankar belonged to the Maihar Gharana (founded by his guru Allauddin Khan), not Agra.

questions.question: Who was awarded the Dadasaheb Phalke Award (highest in Indian cinema) for the year 2023, presented in 2025?
questions.explanation: The Dadasaheb Phalke Award for the year 2023 (India's highest honour in cinema for lifetime contribution) was conferred on legendary Malayalam actor Mohanlal. It was announced in September 2025 and presented to him at the 71st National Film Awards ceremony on 23 September 2025 by President Droupadi Murmu. At 65, he became the youngest recipient ever.

questions.question: Consider the following statements regarding vitamins and their deficiency diseases: 1. A deficiency of Vitamin A (Retinol) leads to night blindness and xerophthalmia 2. A deficiency of Vitamin C (Ascorbic acid) causes scurvy with symptoms like bleeding gums and poor wound healing 3. A deficiency of Vitamin D (Calciferol) results in rickets in children or osteomalacia in adults due to impaired calcium absorption. Which of the above statements is/are correct?
options: Only the statement about Vitamin A is correct
Only the statements about Vitamin C and Vitamin D are correct
None of the statements is correct
questions.explanation: All the given statements are correct. Vitamin A (Retinol) is essential for vision and maintenance of epithelial tissues; its deficiency leads to night blindness due to impaired rhodopsin formation and may also cause xerophthalmia. Vitamin C (Ascorbic acid) is required for proper collagen synthesis, so its deficiency results in scurvy, characterized by bleeding gums and delayed wound healing. Vitamin D (Calciferol) helps in calcium absorption and bone mineralization; therefore, its deficiency causes rickets in children and osteomalacia in adults due to defective bone mineralization.

questions.question: Consider the following pairs regarding Parts and Schedules of the Indian Constitution and their primary subject matters: 1.Part IXB — The Co-operative Societies (added by 97th amendment act) 2.Fifth Schedule — Administration and control of Scheduled Areas and Scheduled Tribes (other than in Assam, Meghalaya, Tripura, Mizoram) 3.Tenth Schedule — Allocation of seats in the Rajya Sabha among States and Union Territories 4.Part IXA — The Municipalities Which of the pairs given above is/are incorrectly matched?
questions.explanation: Pair 1: Correct — Part IXB (Articles 243ZH to 243ZT) deals with Co-operative Societies (inserted by 97th Constitutional Amendment Act, 2011). Pair 2: Correct — Fifth Schedule (under Article 244(1)) provides for administration of Scheduled Areas and Scheduled Tribes in states other than those covered by Sixth Schedule. Pair 3: Incorrect — Tenth Schedule contains Anti-defection provisions (added by 52nd Amendment, 1985; disqualification on grounds of defection for MPs/MLAs). Allocation of seats in Rajya Sabha is in the Fourth Schedule (under Articles 4 and 80). Pair 4: Correct — Part IXA (Articles 243P to 243ZG) deals with Municipalities (added by 74th Amendment, 1992). Hence option B is correct.

questions.question: Which of the following is a trans-Himalayan river that originates north of the main Himalayan range and flows westward before entering India?
questions.explanation: Sutlej is trans-Himalayan (originates from Rakshastal/Mansarovar region north of Himalayas), flows through Tibet then India. Additional information about trans- Himalayan rivers:- Trans-Himalayan rivers originate north of the main Himalayan range (primarily in the Tibetan Plateau) and cut through the Himalayas to enter India or adjacent regions. The three main ones are the Indus, Sutlej, and Brahmaputra. The Indus originates near Lake Mansarovar in Tibet (north of the main Himalayas), but its initial flow in the upper course is northwestward (not strictly westward) through Ladakh (entering India), before bending south-southwest after the Nanga Parbat massif in what is now mostly Pakistan-administered territory. The Brahmaputra (as Yarlung Tsangpo in Tibet) originates north of the Himalayas but flows eastward for much of its course in Tibet before turning south into India. The Sutlej originates from the Rakshastal (or near Mansarovar-Rakas lakes) in southwestern Tibet, north of the main Himalayan range. It flows westward (initially west-northwest, then west and southwest) through Himalayan gorges, entering India via the Shipki La Pass in Himachal Pradesh before continuing into Punjab and eventually joining the Chenab in Pakistan.

questions.question: Which of the following is a major cause of poverty in India?
questions.explanation: The major cause of poverty in India among the given options is lack of education and skills. Education plays a crucial role in enhancing employability, productivity, and income-earning capacity. When individuals lack proper education and vocational skills, they are unable to secure stable and well-paying jobs. This often forces them into informal or low-wage employment, leading to persistent poverty. Low skill levels also reduce opportunities for entrepreneurship and limit access to modern sectors of the economy. Hence option B is correct. Explanation:- a) High literacy rate:- This is not a cause of poverty—in fact, it is the opposite. A high literacy rate (and better education overall) helps reduce poverty by improving employability, productivity, innovation, and access to opportunities. India's progress in raising literacy rates has contributed to poverty reduction over recent decades, though challenges remain in quality and access to education. c) Fast industrialization:- This is not a cause of poverty—rather, it is generally a solution or positive factor. Industrialization creates jobs, boosts economic growth, raises incomes, and helps lift people out of poverty (as seen in India's post-1991 reforms and recent manufacturing push). Slow or inadequate industrialization has historically been cited as a contributor to poverty, not fast industrialization. d) Low population:- This is not a cause of poverty in India. The country faces the opposite issue: rapid/high population growth (population pressure) strains resources, increases competition for jobs/land, contributes to unemployment/underemployment, and exacerbates poverty. Low population would ease pressure on resources and potentially reduce poverty, not cause it.

questions.question: The Nuakhai festival is predominantly observed in which state?
questions.explanation: The Nuakhai festival is predominantly observed in Odisha. It's a harvest festival welcoming new rice. Hence option B is correct. Other festivals of Odisha- konark Festival, Kalinga Festival, Kalinga Festival, Raja Praba, Rath Yatra, Chauu, Bal Yatra.

questions.question: Which pass is known as the "Mountain Pass of Blizzards" due to heavy snowfall and is crucial for Srinagar-Leh highway?
questions.explanation: Zoji La is famous for extreme weather thus it’s called Mountain Pass of Blizzards. Hence option B is correct. Additional information: Passes in Jammu and Kashmir: Karakoram pass, Pir panjal pass, Banihal pass, Burzil pass, Zoji laa pass, Umlignla pass. Passes in Himachal Pradesh: Shipkila pass, Rohtang pass, Bara-Lacha la pass. Passes in Uttarakhand: Mana pass, Niti pass, Lipu Lekh Pass, Mauling La pass.

questions.question: Which of the given statements are correct regarding Swaraj Party and Non Cooperation Movement? 1. Swaraj Party was formed by CR Das and Motilal Nehru. 2. CR Das was the president and Motilal Nehru was one of the secretaries of Swaraj Party. 3. CR Das opposed the Non-Cooperation Movement and left Congress. 4. In the Calcutta session, Congress approved the Non-Cooperation Movement.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Only 1 and 2. Statement 1 is correct because The Swaraj Party was indeed formed by C. R. Das and Motilal Nehru in 1923 after the withdrawal of the Non- Cooperation Movement. Statement 2 is correct because In the party, C. R. Das served as President and Motilal Nehru was one of the secretaries. Statement 3 is incorrect because C. R. Das did not oppose the Non-Cooperation Movement; he supported it but differed on strategy after its withdrawal. Statement 4 is incorrect because the Nagpur Session (1920), not the Calcutta Session, approved the Non- Cooperation Movement. Hence option B is correct.

questions.question: Which of the following statements about the Vibrant Villages Programme-ll (VVP-II) is correct?
options: It is a Centrally Sponsored Scheme implemented by the Ministry of Defence focused on northern border villages.
VVP-II is a Central Sector Scheme with 100% funding from the Centre, covering strategic border villages across multiple states and UTs including Northeast and Northern India.
The programme only focuses on infrastructure development and excludes livelihood generation activities.
VVP-II targets villages along the southern border of India to improve connectivity and tourism.
questions.explanation: Option A is wrong because VVP-II is introduced by Ministry of Home affairs. Option B is correct. Option C is wrong because The programme focuses on infrastructure development and holistic livelihood generation activities. It not excludes livelihoods generation. Option D is wrong because it targets villages along the northern border of India not southern border. Thus option B is correct. Additional information: The Vibrant Villages Programme-II (VVP-II) is a Central Sector Scheme approved by the Union Cabinet on April 2, 2025, with a total outlay of ₹6,839 crore, to be implemented through FY 2028-29. It aims at the comprehensive development of select strategic villages located in blocks abutting international land borders (ILBs) across various Indian states and Union Territories, excluding the northern border clusters which were covered under VVP-I. To ensure safe, secure, and vibrant land borders aligned with the vision of Viksit Bharat@2047.

questions.question: Kepler's First Law of Planetary Motion states that:
options: Planets move in elliptical orbits with the Sun at one focus
Equal areas are swept in equal times
Square of orbital period is proportional to cube of semi-major axis
questions.explanation: Keplar’s planetary laws (1609) 1st law: Planets move in elliptical orbits with the sun as a focus. 2nd law: The line joining the planet and the sun sweep out equal areas in equal interval of time. 3rd law: The square of the time period of revolution of a planet around the sun is directly proportional to the cube of its mean distance from the sun.

questions.question: Which of the following substances is used as a common bleaching agent in households?
questions.explanation: Sodium hypochlorite (NaClO) is the main active ingredient in common household bleach (like brands such as Domex, Rin, or Harpic bleach in India). It is widely used as a bleaching agent to remove stains, whiten clothes, and disinfect surfaces by releasing chlorine, which oxidizes color-causing compounds.

questions.question: The "49th Parallel" is the boundary line between which two countries?
questions.explanation: 49th Parallel line is between USA and Canada. It is the lengthiest international in the world. Hence option is correct B Additional information: Durand Line → Between Pakistan/India and Afghanistan. McMahon Line → Between India and China Radcliffe Line → Between India -Pakistan and India-Bangladesh. 38th Parallel → Between North Korea and South Korea. 49th Parallel → Between USA and Canada Line of Control (LoC) → Between India and Pakistan in Jammu & Kashmir Military control line from 1972 Simla Agreement. Line of Actual Control (LAC) → Between India and China (western & middle sectors)

questions.question: Consider the following statements regarding the Inter-State Council: 1.Article 263 of the Constitution authorizes the Parliament to set up the Inter-State Council. 2.The Council is empowered to inquire into and advise upon disputes arising between States. 3.The Prime Minister acts as its Chairman, and it includes nominated Union Cabinet Ministers as members. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: Statement 1 incorrect because Article 263 empowers the President (not Parliament) to establish the Inter-State Council by order. Statement 2 correct Article 263 explicitly provides that the Council can be charged with the duty of: -Inquiring into and advising upon disputes which may have arisen between States (clause a); -Investigating and discussing subjects in which some or all States (or the Union and States) have common interest (clause b). -Statement 3 is correct The Inter-State Council (as per the 1990 Presidential Order and subsequent amendments) is chaired by the Prime Minister (as Chairman). It also includes six Ministers of Cabinet rank in the Union Council of Ministers nominated by the Prime Minister (along with Chief Ministers of States/UTs, etc.). Hence only statement 2 and 3 are correct Option A is correct.

questions.question: Coins minted in Delhi during the Tomara rule were popularly known as:
questions.explanation: The "Dehliwal" (or "Dehliwal coins") were silver or billon coins minted by the Tomara dynasty (also called Tomars of Delhi) during their rule over Delhi (Dhillika) in the 8th–12th century CE. Hence option B is correct. Addition information: Tanka and Jital: Introduced later by the Delhi Sultanate (e.g., Iltutmish under Mamluks). Silver= Tanka, Copper= jital Rupiya: Silver coin introduced by Sher Shah Suri in the 16th century .

questions.question: Match the following: Waterfall State/Location A) Jog Falls 1) Meghalaya B) Dudhsagar Falls 2) Karnataka C) Nohkalikai Falls 3) Tamil Nadu D) Hogenakal Falls 4) Goa
questions.explanation: Correct Answer: A-2, B-4, C-1, D-3 Jog Falls (also called Gersoppa) → Karnataka, on Sharavati River; one of the highest plunge waterfalls. Dudhsagar Falls → Goa (Mandovi River); famous as "Sea of Milk,". Nohkalikai Falls → Meghalaya (near Cherrapunji). Hogenakal Falls → Tamil Nadu (Cauvery River)

questions.question: Assertion (A): The equilibrium price in a market is determined at the point where quantity demanded equals quantity supplied. Reason (R): At the equilibrium price, there is neither excess demand nor excess supply in the market. Select the correct option:
options: Both (A) and (R) are true, and (R) is the correct explanation of (A).
Both (A) and (R) are true, but (R) is not the correct explanation of (A).
questions.explanation: Assertion (A): The equilibrium price in a market is determined at the point where quantity demanded equals quantity supplied. Reason (R): At the equilibrium price, there is neither excess demand nor excess supply in the market. Correct Option: A → Both A and R are true, and R correctly explains A.

questions.question: Consider the following statements about the Mahatma Gandhi National Rural Employment Guarantee Act (MGNREGA): 1.It was launched in 2005 as a flagship scheme to provide 100 days of guaranteed wage employment in rural areas. 2.Recently, the government proposed to rename and revamp it as "Viksit Bharat – Guarantee for Rozgar and Ajeevika Mission (Gramin)" or VB-G RAM G, increasing workdays to 125 in some cases. 3.The scheme is implemented by the Ministry of Rural Development and is demand-driven. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: -Statement 1 → True: MGNREGA (originally NREGA) started in 2005 under UPA govt. It guarantees 100 days of unskilled manual work per rural household at minimum wage. -Statement 2 → True: In late 2025, the govt introduced a bill to replace/revamp MGNREGA with a new name (VB-G RAM G or similar), dropping "Mahatma Gandhi," and proposed expanding to 125 days in some areas. This was big news due to debates on renaming old schemes and rural jobs. -Statement 3 → True: Ministry of Rural Development handles it; it's "demand-based" – rural households register and can ask for work when needed. Correct Answer: D) 1, 2 and 3

questions.question: Which state became India's largest wool producer in FY24, accounting for nearly 47.5% of the national output?
questions.explanation: Rajasthan topped India's wool production with about 16,013.50 thousand kg (47.5% share), followed by Jammu & Kashmir and Karnataka, Correct Answer: B) Rajasthan

questions.question: Match the following: Event Person/Department A) Most important parallel government during Quit India in terms of long duration 1)Sir Mohammed Iqbal B) Leader who remarked “Nehru is a patriot while Jinnah is a politician” 2)Kadambini Ganguli C) The department of State Correspondence in Delhi Sultanate 3) Satara (Prati Sarkar) D) First woman graduate of Calcutta University to address INC session (1890) 4) Diwan-i-Insha
questions.explanation: Most important parallel government during Quit India in terms of long duration---3) Satara (Prati Sarkar) a.Leader who remarked “Nehru is a patriot while Jinnah is a politician”—1) Sir Mohammed Iqbal b.The department of State Correspondence in Delhi Sultanate—4) Diwan-i-Insha c.First woman graduate of Calcutta University to address INC session (1890)—2) Kadambini Ganguli Hence A-3, B-1, C-4, D-2 is the correct match. Option A is correct.

questions.question: A, B and C start a business. A invests 33(1/3)% of the total capital. B invests 25% of the remaining and C, the rest. A and C left the business after first and second quarter respectively. If the total profit at the end of the year is Rs. 2,16,000 then B’s share is:
questions.explanation: Using, Profit = Investment × Time Let total capital is 6 units. Then as per question, A invests for 3 months, C invests for 6 months and B invests for whole year. Ratio of profits of A, B & C = 2×3 : 1×12∶3×6 = 1: 2 : 3 Share of B in profit = 2/6 × 216000 = 72000 Rs.

questions.question: If 3x+2y-5z = 11, 2x-3y+9z = 14 and y-4z = 15 then find the value of x/2.
questions.explanation: Given that, 3x+2y-5z=11 ………[1] 2x-3y+9z=14 ……….[2] and y-4z=15 ……….[3] add equation [1] and [2] we get, 5x-y+4z=25 = 5x-(y-4z)=25 = 5x-15=25 = 5x = 40 or x = 8 Then required x/2 = 8/2 = 4

questions.question: A garment shop sells a shirt for Rs A, incurring a loss of 5%. During an end-of-season sale, they mark up the same shirt at Rs 1.2A. At the sale, they provide a flat discount of 10% on this marked price. What will be the percentage profit or loss for the garment shop on that shirt during the sale?
questions.explanation: Since 5% loss is involved, let CP = 100 Then SP (first case) = 95. So, A = 95 Marked price during sale = 1.2A = 1.2 × 95 = 114 Apply 10% discount: 10% of 114 = 11.4 SP during sale = 114 − 11.4 = 102.6 Therefore, Profit % = 2.6%

questions.question: A laptop is priced at Rs.40,000. At customer gets a discount of Rs. 4,000 on it. After the discount, the customer buys a mouse marked for Rs. 1,500 at a 20% discount. What is the total amount the customer spends on both items?
questions.explanation: Price of laptop = 40000-4000 = 36000 Price of mouse = 80% of 1500 = 1200 Total amount spent = 36000 + 1200 =Rs 37200

questions.question: The ratio of the time taken by P and Q to complete a task is 3:2. If Q can complete the task alone in 18 hours, how many hours will they take to complete the task if they work together?
questions.explanation: Ratio of time = 3 : 2 Ratio of efficiency = 2 : 3 Total work = 18 × 3 = 54 Time taken by both to complete the work = 54/5 = 10.8 hrs

questions.question: In a class, the ratio of boys to girls is 5 : 3. In an exam, 20% of the boys and 40% of the girls were absent. Among the students who were present, 40% of the boys and 60% of the girls passed the exam. Find the ratio of boys to girls who appeared in the exam and failed.
questions.explanation: Ratio of Boys to Girls = 5 : 3, say 500 and 300 Present boys and girls i.e. 80 % and 60% respectively = 400 and 180 Failed boys and girls, i.e. 60% and 40% respectively = 240 and 72 Required ratio = 240 : 72 = 10 : 3

questions.question: Which of these number sets is NOT a subset of the Real Numbers?
questions.explanation: Real numbers include: • Rational numbers • Irrational numbers • Integers All of these lie on the real number line. Complex numbers are of the form a + ib, where i = √(-1). Since √(-1) is not a real number, complex numbers are not a subset of real numbers.

questions.question: A sum of money is invested at a compound interest rate of 8% p.a. for the first year and 10% p.a. for the second year. If the total interest earned is Rs. 4512, what was the original principal?
questions.explanation: Let principle is 100 units Interest earned in first year = 8 units Interest earned in second year = 10 + 0.8 = 10.8 units Total interest earned = 18.8 units So, 18.8 units = 4512 1 unit = 240 100 units = 24,000 Hence principle is Rs. 24,000

questions.question: A policeman started following a thief who was 1200 metres ahead of him. The thief is running at 2.5 km/h, while the speed of the policeman is 3.75 km/h. Find the distance covered by the thief in metres just when he was caught.
questions.explanation: Initial gap = 1200 m = 1.2 Km Time taken to cover the gap = 1.2/(3.75-2.5) = 1.2/1.25 = 0.96 hours Distance covered by thief = = 2400 m

questions.question: The average of 15 numbers is 80. The average of the first 6 numbers is 72. The average of the next 6 numbers is 25% more than the average of the first 6 numbers. The 13th number is 8 more than the 15th number, and the 14th number is 10 less than the 15th number. What is the average of the 13th and 14th?
questions.explanation: Sum of all 15 numbers = 15×80=1200 Sum of first 6 numbers = 6×72=432 Sum of next 6 numbers = 6×72×5/4 = 540 Sum of 13th, 14th and 15th numbers = 1200-540-432 = 228 Let 15th number be a. then 13th number be (a+8) and 14th number be (a-10). Then sum = a+a+8+a-10 = 3a-2 = 228 = 3a = 230 or a = 230/3 = 76(2/3) Required average = (a+8+a-10)/2 = (2a-2)/2 = a-1 = 76(2/3)-1 = 75(2/3) = 75.67

questions.question: At present, Himanshu is younger than Bipin by 8 years. If 4 years ago, ages of Himanshu, Bipin and Ranjeet were in the ratio 1:2:3 then what is the present age of Ranjeet in years?
questions.explanation: Ratio of ages of Himanshu, Bipin and Ranjeet = 1 : 2 : 3 Since, Himanshu is younger than Bipin by 8 years at present, so 4 years ago, he was younger than Bipin by 8 years too. So, the difference between the ratio = (2-1) unit = 8 1 unit = 8 So, 4 years ago, the age of Himanshu, Bipin and Ranjeet = 8 years, 16 years, 24 years Present age of Ranjeet = 24+4 = 28 years

questions.question: The following data set is given: 8, 12, 15, 13, 12, 12, 14, 10 Which of the following statements is correct regarding the distribution?
questions.explanation: Given data: 8,12,15,13,12,12,14,10 Mean = sum/(total numbers) = 96/8 = 12 Mode = 12 Skewness = (Mean-Mode)/(Standard deviation) = (12-12)/(Standard deviation) = 0 So, if the mode and mean of a data is equal then the skewness always be zero and the distribution is symmetric.

questions.question: P and Q together can do a piece of work in 20 days. They worked for 8 days, and M completed the remaining in 4 days. How long will M alone take to do the whole work?
questions.explanation: Part of work completed in 8 days by P and Q together = 8/20 = 2/5 Remaining work (1-2/5) = 3/5 completed by M in 4 days. So, time taken by M to complete the whole work alone = 5×4/3 = 20/3 = 6(1/3) days

questions.question: A man rows a boat to a point 45 km upstream and returns to the starting point. The total time taken for the entire journey is 14 hours. The speed of the stream is 2 km/h. if the stream speed increases by 1 km/h, how much additional time (in hours) will the man take to complete the same journey
questions.explanation: Let speed of the Boat = x km/h Speed of the stream = 2 km/h Then as per question, = 45/(x+2) + 45/(x-2) = 14…..[1] = x=7 satisfies the equation Hence speed of the boat = 7 km/h Now, if speed of the stream increases by 1 km/h New Speed of stream = 3 km/h Then, New time = 45/10 + 45/4=4.5+11.25 = 15.75 hrs Therefore, Extra time = 15.75-14 = 1.75 hours

questions.question: Find a single discount equivalent to the successive discount of 20%, 24%, and 32%.
questions.explanation: Equivalent discount of two successive discount of 20% and 24% = 20+24-(20×24)/100 = 44-4.8=39.2 Equivalent discount of two successive discount of 39.2% and 32% = 39.2+32-(39.2×32)/100 = 71.2-12.54 = 58.66

questions.question: A mixture containing 72% oil is mixed with another mixture containing 48% oil in the ratio 6:16. What is the percentage of oil in the mixture formed?
questions.explanation: Using weighted average = {(72×6+48×16)/22}×100 = 54.55%

E.g. 13 – Operations on 13 such as adding /subtracting /multiplying to 13 can be performed. Breaking down 13 into 1 and 3 and then performing mathematical operations on 1 and 3 is not allowed.)

In this question, a statement is followed by two conclusions, numbered I and II. Decide which of the two conclusions is/are true based on the given statement. Statement: L≤ S = P > Q ≥ R ≥ B Conclusions: I. S ≥ B II. T>R
questions.explanation: Sol: S ≥ B → wrong S >B (single sign is correct) II → T >R→ WRONG (opposite signs in between)

questions.question: 8 people are standing in a row facing north. Two people are sitting between A and G. G is not sitting on either end of the row. G and B are sitting adjacent to each other. H is sitting third from the left end. C is sitting fifth to the left of F and does not sit on end of the row. D sit at extreme right end of the row. One people is sitting between B and F. Who is sitting between B and F?
questions.explanation: ![](https://www.thepunditsofficial.in/admin/assets/uploads/ckeditor/3d9e41772885609669.png) E is sitting in between B and F

questions.question: Two statements, 1 and 2 have been given. These statements may be independent causes or effects of independent causes or a common cause. One of the statements may be the effect of the other statement. Read both the statements and select the correct option. 1) Police authority has recently increased vigilance in the locality ‘X’. 2) There has been significant reduction in petty crimes in the locality ‘X’.
options: 1 is the cause and 2 is the possible effect
2 is the cause and 1 is the possible effect
Both 1 and 2 are effects of independent causes
questions.explanation: Increased police vigilance is a proactive measure designed to deter criminal activity (Reflected in the statement 1). When police authority increases patrols and monitoring in a specific area, it creates a deterrent environment, which logically leads to a reduction in crimes (Reflected in the statement 2). The reduction in petty crimes is the direct positive outcome of the increased vigilance. Hence, 1 is the cause and 2 is the possible effect.

questions.explanation: Private discussion + cooperative task‑sharing addresses the issue without conflict escalation.

questions.question: Select the option that is related to the fifth number in the same way as the second number is related to the first number and the fourth number is related to the third number. 16: 576 :: 12 : 324 :: 18 : ?
In a certain code language, ‘MONK’ is coded as ‘57’ and ‘TRAMP’ is coded as ‘73’. How will ‘LAMENT’ be coded in the same language?
questions.explanation: Sol: Logic Sum of place value + Number of letters MONK = 13+15+14+11 = 53+4= 57 TRAMP= 20+18+1+13+16=68+5=73 LAMENT= 12+1+13+5+14+20=65+6=71
